task_completion.description: Format the title on the first line as bold and underlined.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the first line of text in the Word document. It should be visually bolded and have an underline. The Bold ('B') and Underline ('U') buttons on the Word ribbon may also appear highlighted if the cursor is on the title.

task_completion.description: Apply subscript formatting to numbers within chemical formulas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the chemical formulas in the text (C7H6O3, C4H6O3, C9H8O4, C2H4O2, H2SO4). The numbers within these formulas must be formatted as subscripts (smaller and set below the text baseline). The subscript button ('x₂') on the Home ribbon might be seen being used.

task_completion.description: Ensure quantities and time values remain unsubscripted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at regular numbers in the document, such as quantities (e.g., grams, mL) or time values (e.g., minutes). These numbers must remain in standard formatting (normal size, on the baseline) and NOT be subscripted.

task_completion.description: Replace all instances of 'deg C' with the degree symbol and C.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for temperature values in the text. The text 'deg C' should be replaced with the actual degree symbol followed by C (e.g., '85°C'). There should be 2 instances of this.

Look for the 'Save As' dialog box showing the file being saved as 'Lab_Report_Formatted.docx' in the Documents folder, or look at the top title bar of the Word window in the final screenshots to see if the document name has changed to 'Lab_Report_Formatted.docx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Microsoft Word 2010 GUI to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is interacting with the Word ribbon (Home tab, Font section) to apply Bold, Underline, and Subscript formatting, rather than using a terminal script or alternative text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actively edited the text to replace 'deg C' with '°C'.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent typing the degree symbol, using the 'Insert Symbol' dialog, or using the 'Find and Replace' feature in Word to change 'deg C' to '°C'.

integrity.description: The agent used Word's save functionality to create the new file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the 'File' menu -> 'Save As' or the Save icon, rather than terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv' to rename the file outside of Word.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires formatting a chemistry lab report about aspirin synthesis in Microsoft Word 2010. The specific chemical formulas that must have subscripted numbers are: C7H6O3 (salicylic acid), C4H6O3 (acetic anhydride), C9H8O4 (aspirin), C2H4O2 (acetic acid), and H2SO4 (sulfuric acid). There are exactly 2 instances of 'deg C' that need to be replaced with '°C'. Ensure the agent does not accidentally subscript regular numbers like quantities or times.
